issue: Skipped rules surface generic "No role/stack match" reasons even when a richer signal exists (e.g. a conflicting ecosystem present, or a near-miss requiresAny entry). The explain layer cannot tell users *why* a particular rule was rejected versus simply not applicable.
expected: Skip reasons should distinguish: (a) hard policy block (unsupported, sensitive, source-trust), (b) required role/dep missing with the specific signal name, (c) conflicting ecosystem detected, (d) score below threshold with the actual score.
rootCauseHypothesis: pushSkipReason is only called for explicit policy paths in src/recommender/recommend.ts; the fallback `no-role-stack-match` is generic and does not record the closest signal the rule would have needed.
resolvedByTest: tests/context-explain.test.js (explain-recommendation/context human output includes signal annotations)
